transcript: Listen, hamburger isn't just born as a mass of meat in a plastic container. You can walk up to the butcher and say that dry aged ribeye steak, that's mine, I'm going to grind that up into my burger. You're in total control. The signature burger of Minneapolis. It's a cheeseburger where the cheese is in the center of the burger. It's molecular gastronomy at its finest. Ladies and gentlemen, the Juicy Lucy. There's so much more to a great burger than just buying pre-made patties at the supermarket, right? Where you're just looking for maybe lean to fat ratio. It's about picking different cuts of meat to achieve different flavors. And that's how we're gonna really make this our own burger. For this burger, we have some short rib, which is fatty and lean. It's got a great mouthfeel. It's really luscious. So this is a dry-aged ribeye steak. It's what everyone who knows how to order steak orders at a steakhouse. And we just trim the fat off of it, and we're gonna grind that up. And we have also chuck, and chuck is that cut that it's inexpensive, but it's what everyone thinks of when they eat a burger. So we have this dry-aged funk from this ribeye steak. We have this luscious short rib. And then we have the chuck, which is everyone's familiar with that. So real men grind their own meat. And there's something sort of carnal about throwing meat through a grinder here. So, now we have our meat ground. We have our chuck, our short rib, and our dry-aged ribeye fat in here. Now, we're going to get to making our patty. So, we're going to form two patties. All right. So, the bottom layer here, we're going to throw into the can. So, Juicy Lucy is actually from Minneapolis. Neat thing about it, all the restaurants do it out there. The cheese is in the middle of the cheeseburger. Now we're going to form our second patty. Make the other side of this ravioli. This is our Juicy Lucy. We have the short rib, the chuck, the dry-aged ribeye, and then hidden inside of all of this meaty deliciousness is the white melted American cheese. Well, will be melted once we cook it and that's what we're gonna do. The technique that we're gonna use for cooking this burger, same technique you would use if you were cooking a big steak and we're gonna add a little bit of oil or fat to our pan. In this case it's a little bit of duck fat. Duck fat is also a crucial ingredient in my hair recipe, which is equal parts nitrogen and duck fat you like that we want to get a nice sear on this okay it's called the maillard reaction what makes you walk by a grill and go oh my god that smells so delicious one of the mistakes people make again whether they're cooking fish burgers steak whatever it is they want to mess around with it too much right and we're just going to let it cook and do its thing so now we're getting ready to flip our burger i'm going to be really careful it looks nice and brown and now we want to add some aromatics we're going to add some other flavor to it thyme and rosemary smash up some garlic and for good measure, we'll throw in butter as well. And just by them being in the pan, their flavor is going to go into the burger. Now this is what's happening in the fancy three-star restaurant when you order a big steak or a piece of fish. By basting it, we're making sure that all that flavor gets into the burger and that it doesn't dry out. One of the things about cooking the Juicy Lucy, you want to be careful that you don't have a blowout, that the cheese doesn't come out. That's why we're cooking it a little gentler. You know, this is tech meat. There's a lot that's going into this, and all of a sudden, this is fine dining meat. So we're gonna let our burger rest, kinda hang out. It's in a sort of butter jacuzzi over there. Now we're gonna get into our bun. So this is brioche. All right, brioche is an egg and butter enriched bread. I like to cut a little bit more towards the top of it because the burger's kind of heavy. We're going to put a few condiments on there. So this could be the difference between your bun and your burger falling apart or a burger that holds its integrity the last bite. Now we're going to toast our bun in the same pan that we made the burger. Why not mop up all of this good, delicious juices one more last time? And now we're gonna start plating. I'm a purist, I love ketchup. One of my favorite condiments. So now we're gonna put our caramelized onions on top. We've just taken some raw onions, we've thrown it into a pot with a little bit of oil, and we've just cooked it very, very slow till it gets nice and dark. So this is like pure, unadulterated flavor. I'm a big pickle fan. Again, acidity really wakes up a dish. That's looking delicious. So this burger's got everything on it. Gushing with cheese, the Juicy Lucy. Gushing cheese is so good. Make this at home and tell me what you think in the comments. Don't screw up Valentine's Day this year. Surprise that special someone with the ultimate pleasure, Nutella butter. You'd have to be crazy to live off vending machine food, unless it's coming from one of these five super machines. The Greeks might not know how to balance a budget, but they sure can make a sandwich. Click here and learn how to make a souvlaki sammy. Check out Furious Pete, Chris Thompson, and John from Simply Sp spoons as they and the gang get hurly-burly with some haggis subscribe for more free tasted treats
